name: Maneaters
source: Border Town Burning — Maneaters warband list, fetched from https://www.broheim.net/downloads/warbands/supplement/btb/Maneaters.pdf on 2026-07-03. Owner should still cross-check against their own BTB PDF/printing for errata.

heroSlots.notes: 145 gc to hire. Leader: any warrior within 6" of the Captain may use his Leadership when taking Ld tests. Large (causes fear, valid ranged target with +1 to hit even if not closest). Ogres never carry/use daggers — no free starting dagger. Only the Captain may buy the Cathayan longsword from the Ogre equipment list.

145 gc to hire. Uses the Guide equipment list, not the Ogre equipment list. Large. Ranger: if not taken out of action, roll two dice for Exploration and pick one (not a re-roll). Loner: never claims a Gnoblar, immune to All Alone tests, may never become warband leader. Required for the warband to be allowed to include Sabretusks (0-2 Sabretusks 'if it includes a Guide').

45 gc to hire. Explicitly exempt from Fear and Large (does not cause fear, not a Large target) — the only Hero-slot Ogre that is not Large. May not buy the Cathayan longsword (Captain only). The 'maneater' special skill (learn a Shooting/Academic skill) may not be taken by the Guide, implying it is intended for Captain/Youngblood.

henchmenTypes.notes: 140 gc to hire, 0-2 per warband. Bull Charge: when charging may attempt a single attack at +1 to hit instead of normal attacks; on a hit the target is automatically knocked down. Large (full Ogre). No free dagger.
85 gc to hire, unlimited number allowed ('any number of Half-growns'). Explicitly exempt from Large, same as Youngbloods. No free dagger.

125 gc to hire, 0-2 per warband, ONLY allowed if the warband includes a Mountain Guide. Weapons/Armour: none — fixed 'Tusks and primal ferocity'. Trained: uses the Mountain Guide's Leadership within 6"; if the warband has no Guide, Sabretusks are caged and unusable until one is hired. Feral Instinct: must pass a Ld test at the start of the Ogre turn or the opponent controls it that turn (it may even charge its own warband). Ignored: does not count toward out-of-action totals for Rout tests. Fear: causes fear. TODO: verify vs Border Town Burning whether Sabretusks are treated as an 'animal' that cannot earn XP/advances — the source PDF gives them no starting XP value and no stat-maximum row, but does not explicitly state the animal-XP rule the way the core rulebook does for Warhounds/Dogs. isAnimal is currently set to true as the best guess; confirm before relying on it. statMaximums are not given in the source and are left null pending verification.

exclusiveEquipment.name: Ogre club
exclusiveEquipment.rulesText: Range: Close Combat; Strength: as user. Special rules — Concussion: a roll of 2-4 on the Injury table is treated as Stunned when this weapon is used. Crushing Attack: imposes -1 to enemy armour saves and the wielder's Strength counts as one higher for the defender's parry attempts; only applies when wielded two-handed.

exclusiveEquipment.name: Iron fist
exclusiveEquipment.rulesText: Range: Close Combat; Strength: as user. Special rules — Parry. Gloved: cannot hold another weapon (e.g. a double-handed weapon) in that hand; two iron fists means no other close combat weapons may be used. Dual-role: re-roll failed parry attempts if paired with a sword or another iron fist.

exclusiveEquipment.name: Cathayan longsword
exclusiveEquipment.rulesText: TODO: verify vs Border Town Burning p.XX — cost (75 gc) and Captain-only restriction are confirmed from the Maneaters equipment list, but this document did not include the weapon's full profile/special rules text.

exclusiveEquipment.name: Hand-held mortar
exclusiveEquipment.rulesText: Cost: 80 + 2D6 gc. Range: 24"; Strength: 4. Special rules — Move or fire, Prepare shot, Scatter (misses land 2D6" in a random direction), Experimental (subject to optional Blackpowder Weapons rules; anything but 'BOOM!' jams/empties the weapon), Explosive Radius (target and anything within 1.5" of the final landing spot takes a single S4 hit).

exclusiveEquipment.name: Harpoon crossbow
exclusiveEquipment.rulesText: Range: 30"; Strength: 5. Special rules — Move or fire, Prepare shot.

exclusiveEquipment.name: Lookout-Gnoblar
exclusiveEquipment.restriction: Rare 8 (Ogres only). Not a model — counts as equipment, does not occupy a base. Max 2 different Claimed Gnoblars per Ogre.
exclusiveEquipment.rulesText: The owning Ogre gains the Dodge skill from the Speed skill list. If the Ogre is taken out of action, roll a D6 for each Claimed Gnoblar he owns: on a 1-2 it is dead and removed from his equipment.

exclusiveEquipment.name: Luck-Gnoblar
exclusiveEquipment.restriction: Rare 9 (Ogres only). Not a model — counts as equipment, does not occupy a base. Max 2 different Claimed Gnoblars per Ogre.
exclusiveEquipment.rulesText: The owning Ogre may re-roll one dice during the battle (never re-roll a re-roll). If the Ogre is taken out of action, roll a D6 for each Claimed Gnoblar he owns: on a 1-2 it is dead and removed from his equipment.

exclusiveEquipment.name: Sword-Gnoblar
exclusiveEquipment.restriction: Rare 10 (Ogres only). Not a model — counts as equipment, does not occupy a base. Max 2 different Claimed Gnoblars per Ogre.
exclusiveEquipment.rulesText: The owning Ogre gains one extra Strength 2 attack in close combat at the owning model's Weapon Skill, made against a model the Ogre is already directing attacks toward. If the Ogre is taken out of action, roll a D6 for each Claimed Gnoblar he owns: on a 1-2 it is dead and removed from his equipment.

exclusiveEquipment.name: Gnoblar Fighter
exclusiveEquipment.restriction: TODO: verify vs Border Town Burning p.XX — listed at 15 gc on the Ogre equipment list's Miscellaneous section but its rules text was not present on the fetched pages (only Lookout/Luck/Sword-Gnoblar rules text was given under 'Claimed Gnoblars').
exclusiveEquipment.rulesText: TODO: verify vs Border Town Burning p.XX

specialRules: Fear: Ogres are large, threatening creatures that cause fear, with the exception of Youngbloods. | Large: except for Youngbloods and Half-growns, Ogres are huge and make tempting targets for archers — any model may shoot at them even if not the closest target, at +1 to hit. | Gluttony: each Ogre model counts as two models when selling wyrdstone or treasure. A model captured (Serious Injuries or Exploration) can instead be devoured, reducing the combined model count of your warband by one (two if the eaten captive is a Large Target); an Ogre always counts as at least one model no matter how much he eats. An Ogre Hero devouring captured models gains XP equal to the number of models consumed; any warband member or animal (including mounts) can be eaten this way and is removed from the roster immediately. | Slow Witted: Ogres only improve at half the normal rate — they need twice the usual XP to gain an advance. | Difficult Customers: Ogre Heroes suffer -1 when rolling to find Rare items that are not exclusively available to Ogres. | Cannibals: an Ogre warband may never hire Hired Swords except Halflings (Scout, Thief, etc.) and the Ogre Bodyguard (or unless stated otherwise), and may choose to devour a Hired Sword when his contract ends. | Minimum warband size is 3 models; maximum warband size is 12. | Characteristic maximums for all Ogre-kin (Captain, Mountain Guide, Youngblood, Bulls, Half-growns) are M6 WS6 BS5 S5 T5 W5 I6 A5 Ld9 — Sabretusks are not Ogres and have no stated maximum profile in this source. | Ogres never carry or fight with daggers; they receive no free starting dagger unlike other warbands' warriors.
